Implementation.Explanation:
The GridMapModule (CMP--3) has been updated to explicitly describe how cells become FREE and BLOCKED, tying the behavior directly to LL--MAP--1.4.3 and LL--MAP--1.4.4.
Concretely: - In CMP--3.Execution: * A dedicated "Free cell confirmation" step was added: - On CellCenterEntryEvent from PoseEstimation, if the target cell is UNKNOWN, MotionControlModule indicates that the transition into the cell completed without collision or emergency stop on the entry face, and the Reliability/consistency module reports no contradictory evidence for that cell, then the GridMapModule performs UNKNOWN->FREE for that cell and emits a classification-change event. This mirrors LL--MAP--1.4.3’s wording: “successfully traversed into the cell without detecting a collision or blocking wall on the entry face, and no contradictory evidence”. * A dedicated "Blocked cell inference" step was added: - On WallObservation reporting a PRESENT wall between the current cell and a neighbor, GridMapModule: + Sets the corresponding wall segment from UNKNOWN to PRESENT (if UNKNOWN). + Sets the neighbor cell from UNKNOWN to BLOCKED (if UNKNOWN) and emits classification-change events, reflecting LL--MAP--1.4.4. - In CMP--3.Invariants: * We now explicitly state that: - UNKNOWN->FREE transitions are driven by MotionControlModule execution completion and consistency info. - UNKNOWN->BLOCKED transitions are driven by WallDetection/Mapping’s WallObservation (PRESENT wall to neighbor). These changes fully specify where and how a cell becomes FREE or BLOCKED in line with the referenced LLRs.

The DerivedFrom list for the Frontier Generation and Exploration Target Selection component (CMP--2, previously CMP--3 in the finding) has been corrected.
Changes: - The duplicate “LL--MAP--1.1.6” entry was removed. - The reference to LL--MAP--1.1.6 was removed entirely from CMP--2.DerivedFrom, since this component does not implement the map completeness criterion or provide a completeness Boolean. - LL--MAP--1.1.6 remains referenced only by: * CMP--3 (Grid Map Module), which computes and exposes GM_MapCompletenessFlag. * CMP--11 (Exploration Status and Time Limit Module), which uses the completeness flag to emit EXPLORATION_COMPLETED events. The updated CMP--2.DerivedFrom now lists only the requirements directly implemented by frontier generation and target selection (LL--MAP--1.1.1, 1.1.3, 1.1.4, 1.1.5, 1.1.6 as Frontier Generation, 1.3.1, 1.5.2, 1.5.3, and LL--PLAN--1.1.1/1.1.2).

The Mapping and Wall Detection Module (CMP--6, previously referred to as CMP--10 in the finding) execution description has been clarified to align unequivocally with the static map assumption.
Changes: - In CMP--6.Execution, the “Consistency handling” step was rewritten: * For UNKNOWN entries: - N consecutive consistent readings are used to decide the first classification, i.e., whether to issue MM_MapUpdateRequest (UNKNOWN->PRESENT/ABSENT for walls, UNKNOWN->FREE/BLOCKED for cells). * For already classified entries (FREE/BLOCKED or PRESENT/ABSENT): - Any contradictory observation is treated purely as sensor error; the module does not issue MM_MapUpdateRequest that would attempt to change FREE<->BLOCKED or PRESENT<->ABSENT or revert to UNKNOWN. - In CMP--6.Invariants: * We added explicit statements: - “For map entries already classified (non-UNKNOWN), mapping never requests a new classification that would change FREE↔BLOCKED or PRESENT↔ABSENT.” - AdditionalData.DAT--5 now clarifies that N is only used while entries are UNKNOWN; once they become FREE/BLOCKED or PRESENT/ABSENT, immutability holds and N is not used to override them. This removes the previous ambiguity and fully complies with LL--MAP--1.3.5, LL--MAP--1.4.1, and LL--MAP--1.4.2.

The Motion Control and Collision Avoidance component (CMP--8, previously CMP--11 in the finding) has been extended to explicitly encode the “no resume until new safe trajectory” constraint from LL--RELIAB--1.1.2.
Changes: - An internal EMERGENCY_STOPPED mode/state was introduced in CMP--8.Execution: * When emergency conditions are detected, Motion Control: - Immediately calls motor_set(0,0). - Sets internal state to EMERGENCY_STOPPED. - Reports path execution aborted and raises an EmergencyStopEvent. * While in EMERGENCY_STOPPED: - The module does not advance any motion primitives. - It does not issue any nonzero motor_set commands. - It remains in this mode until a new MC_MotionCommand representing a safe trajectory is received and explicitly accepted, at which point the state returns to NORMAL (or AVOIDANCE if appropriate). - CMP--8.Invariants now include: * “Following an EmergencyStopEvent (transition to EMERGENCY_STOPPED), motor speeds remain at 0 and no motion primitives are advanced until a new MC_MotionCommand representing a safe trajectory is received and accepted.” This makes the LL--RELIAB--1.1.2 requirement explicit in the design, not only at higher-level mission logic but in the actuator control component itself.

The determinism requirement has been made explicit in FSM--1, the artifact directly representing the top-level mission control state machine.
Changes: - In CMP--1.Invariants: * We extended the invariant to state: - “State transitions are deterministic for a given history of events and time; in each control-loop cycle, for any combination of evaluated inputs/events, at most one outgoing transition from the current state is taken, with fixed priority rules when multiple triggers are simultaneously true.” - This ties CMP--1 determinism to FSM--1, which defines all states and transitions; while FSM--1’s structure already implied this, the invariant now explicitly documents the guarantee required by LL--MAP--1.1.1. - FSM--1 remains the formal behavior definition; the added invariant ensures the state machine itself is documented as deterministic for any combination of state and inputs/events per cycle, in line with LL--MAP--1.1.1’s “deterministic transitions”.

The naming inconsistency between the enums in DAT--1 and the COMPLETED state description in the MissionControlStateMachine has been resolved by harmonizing on the more specific names.
Changes: - In DAT--1.Content: * ReturnToStartStatusEnum is defined as: enum ReturnToStartStatusEnum { RETURN_NOT_EXECUTED = 0, RETURN_SUCCESS = 1, RETURN_FAILURE = 2 }; * SecondRunStatusEnum is defined as: enum SecondRunStatusEnum { SECOND_RUN_NOT_EXECUTED = 0, SECOND_RUN_SUCCESS = 1, SECOND_RUN_FAILURE = 2 }; - In CMP--1 (Mission Control State Machine), COMPLETED state description and MC_SecondRunStatusOutput were updated to use: * SECOND_RUN_NOT_EXECUTED (instead of generic NOT_EXECUTED). * Similarly, ReturnToStartStatus is aligned conceptually with RETURN_NOT_EXECUTED/RETURN_SUCCESS/RETURN_FAILURE. This ensures that the textual description and enum definitions use the same spelling and semantics, removing ambiguity when implementing or reporting second run and return-to-start statuses.

The explicit trace between CMP--1 and FSM--1 has been added, making the ownership relationship unambiguous.
Changes: - In CMP--1.Purpose: * The first sentence now states: “Central mission coordinator that owns the top-level mission state machine (defined formally in FSM--1) and orchestrates exploration, return-to-start, and second run behaviors.” - In CMP--1.Execution: * The final sentence now reads: “The behavior of this component, including all state transitions and their guards, is fully specified by state machine FSM--1 in the StateMachines artifact.” These additions explicitly link CMP--1 to FSM--1 and make clear that LL--MAP--1.1.1, LL--MAP--1.1.2, and LL--RUN--1.2.1 are satisfied via the state machine described in FSM--1, satisfying the SDP traceability requirement.
